title: nymphaea occidentalis
intro: Nymphaea occidentalis, also known as the Western Water Lily or Yellow Water Lily, is a perennial aquatic plant native to North America. It is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the family Nymphaeaceae, characterized by its large, flat leaves and showy yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Nymphaea occidentalis include teas and infusions, which are used to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote overall well-being, while also being used as a natural remedy for skin conditions and digestive issues.
